web_text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ our hope; to Timothy, my true child in faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord. As I urged you when I was going into Macedonia, stay at Ephesus that you might command certain men not to teach a different doctrine, and not to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which cause disputes, rather than God’s stewardship, which is in faith—
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle of Jesus Christ, actin' on orders from God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ, our hope; writin' to Timothy, my true kid in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord. Same as I pushed you when I was headed to Macedonia, stick around in Ephesus so you can order certain guys to quit teachin' bogus doctrine, and quit obsessin' over myths and endless family trees, which just stir up arguments instead of God's plan, which runs on faith —

web_text: but the goal of this command is love, out of a pure heart and a good conscience and sincere faith; from which things some, having missed the mark, have turned away to vain talking; desiring to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they say, nor about what they strongly affirm. But we know that the law is good, if a person uses it lawfully,
slang_text: the whole point of this order is love, comin' from a pure heart, a clean conscience, and genuine faith. Some folks missed the mark on that and wandered off into empty talk, tryin' to act like teachers of the law when they don't even understand what they're sayin', or what they're so dead-set on arguin' about. But we know the law's good, long as somebody's usin' it the right way,

web_text: as knowing this, that law is not made for a righteous person, but for the lawless and insubordinate, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers, for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for slave-traders, for liars, for perjurers, and for any other thing contrary to the sound doctrine; according to the Good News of the glory of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust. And I thank him who enabled me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he counted me faithful, appointing me to service;
slang_text: knowin' this: the law's not made for righteous folks, it's made for lawless, rebellious types, for ungodly sinners, for unholy, foul-mouthed people, for folks who kill their own parents, for murderers, for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for slave-traders, for liars, for folks who lie under oath, and any other bogus thing that goes against sound doctrine — matchin' up with the Good News about the glory of the blessed God, which got handed to me to look after. And I'm thankin' the one who gave me the strength for this, Christ Jesus our Lord, 'cause He counted me faithful and put me to work;

web_text: although I was before a blasphemer, a persecutor, and insolent. However, I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief. The grace of our Lord abounded exceedingly with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus. The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief. However, for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first, Jesus Christ might display all his patience, for an example of those who were going to believe in him for eternal life.
slang_text: even though I used to be a blasphemer, a persecutor, straight-up insolent about it. But I got shown mercy, 'cause I was actin' clueless, still in unbelief. And our Lord's grace overflowed way beyond that, loaded with faith and love, all through Christ Jesus. Here's a sayin' you can trust, worth acceptin' all the way: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners — and I'm the worst one out there. But that's exactly why I got shown mercy, so in me first, Jesus Christ could show off all His patience, as an example for everybody who's ever gonna believe in Him for eternal life.

web_text: Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen. This instruction I commit to you, my child Timothy, according to the prophecies which were given to you before, that by them you may wage the good warfare; holding faith and a good conscience; which some having thrust away made a shipwreck concerning the faith; of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I delivered to Satan, that they might be taught not to blaspheme.
slang_text: Now to the King who's eternal, immortal, invisible, to the one and only wise God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen. I'm handin' you this instruction, my kid Timothy, matchin' up with the prophecies you already got, so you can fight the good fight usin' 'em — holdin' onto faith and a clean conscience. Some folks tossed that stuff aside and wrecked their faith completely, like a ship goin' down — guys like Hymenaeus and Alexander, who I handed over to Satan, so they'd learn not to blaspheme no more.

web_text: I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks, be made for all men: for kings and all who are in high places; that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence. For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior; who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.
slang_text: So first off, I'm pushin' you to make sure requests, prayers, pleadin' for others, and givin' thanks all get made for everybody — for kings and everybody in high positions — so we can live a peaceful, quiet life, all godly and reverent. That's good, and it's exactly what pleases God our Savior, who wants every single person saved and full-up on the truth.

web_text: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself as a ransom for all; the testimony in its own times; to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth. I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.
slang_text: 'Cause there's one God, and one middleman between God and people — the man Christ Jesus, who handed Himself over as a ransom for everybody; a testimony given right on time. That's what I got appointed a preacher and apostle for (I'm tellin' you the truth in Christ, no lie), a teacher to the Gentiles in faith and truth. So I want the guys everywhere to pray, liftin' up holy hands, no anger, no arguin'.

web_text: In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety; not just with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing; but (which becomes women professing godliness) with good works. Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission. But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.
slang_text: Same deal, women should dress modest and proper, not just chasin' fancy braids, gold, pearls, or expensive threads — but instead (fittin' for women who claim to be godly) dressed up in good deeds. Let a woman learn quietly, all the way submissive. I'm not lettin' a woman teach or boss around a man — she oughta stay quiet.

web_text: For Adam was first formed, then Eve. Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience; but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.
slang_text: 'Cause Adam got formed first, then Eve. Adam didn't get conned, but the woman got conned and fell into disobedience. But she'll get saved through havin' kids, long as they keep stickin' with faith, love, and holiness, stayin' level-headed about it.

web_text: This is a faithful saying: if a man seeks the office of an overseer, he desires a good work. The overseer therefore must be without reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, sensible, modest, hospitable, good at teaching; not a drinker, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous; one who rules his own house well, having children in subjection with all reverence;
slang_text: Here's a sayin' you can trust: if a guy's chasin' after the overseer job, he's after a good gig. So the overseer's gotta be beyond reproach, one wife's husband, level-headed, sharp, modest, hospitable, good at teachin'; not a drunk, not violent, not chasin' money, but gentle, not pickin' fights, not greedy; runnin' his own household well, keepin' his kids in line with respect;

web_text: (but if a man doesn’t know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of the assembly of God?) not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the same condemnation as the devil. Moreover he must have good testimony from those who are outside, to avoid falling into reproach and the snare of the devil. Servants, in the same way, must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for money;
slang_text: (but if a guy can't even run his own house, how's he gonna take care of God's church?) and not some fresh convert, or he might get puffed up and land in the same condemnation as the devil. On top of that, he's gotta have a solid reputation with the folks outside the church, so he doesn't fall into disgrace and get snagged by the devil's trap. Deacons, same deal, gotta be respectable, not two-faced, not hooked on wine, not chasin' money;

web_text: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience. Let them also first be tested; then let them serve if they are blameless. Their wives in the same way must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things. Let servants be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
slang_text: holdin' onto the mystery of the faith with a clean conscience. Test 'em out first, and if they come out blameless, let 'em serve. Their wives, same deal, gotta be respectable, no trash-talkin', level-headed, faithful across the board. Let the deacons be one-wife husbands, runnin' their kids and their own households well.

web_text:
For those who have served well gain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus. These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly; but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth. Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great:
God was revealed in the flesh,
justified in the spirit,
seen by angels,
preached among the nations,
believed on in the world,
and received up in glory.
slang_text:
'Cause the ones who serve well earn themselves a solid reputation, and huge boldness in the faith that's in Christ Jesus. I'm writin' you all this, hopin' to get to you real soon, but just in case I get held up, I want you to know how folks oughta act in God's house — that's the church of the livin' God, the pillar and foundation of the truth. No arguin' about it, the mystery of godliness is epic:
God showed up in the flesh,
proved right in the Spirit,
seen by angels,
preached to every nation,
believed on all over the world,
and taken back up in glory.

web_text: But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to seducing spirits and doctrines of demons, through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies, branded in their own conscience as with a hot iron; forbidding marriage and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth. For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, if it is received with thanksgiving.
slang_text: But the Spirit's sayin' it flat out: down the road, some folks are gonna bail on the faith, chasin' after con-artist spirits and demon doctrines, through two-faced liars whose own conscience is branded over like it got hit with a hot iron; banning marriage and orderin' folks to skip foods that God made to be enjoyed with thanks by believers who know the truth. 'Cause everything God made is good, and nothin's off-limits, long as you take it with thanksgiving.

web_text: For it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer. If you instruct the brothers of these things, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith, and of the good doctrine which you have followed. But refuse profane and old wives’ fables. Exercise yourself toward godliness. For bodily exercise has some value, but godliness has value in all things, having the promise of the life which is now, and of that which is to come.
slang_text: 'Cause it gets made holy through God's word and prayer. If you teach the brothers this stuff, you're gonna turn into a good servant of Christ Jesus, fed on the words of faith and the solid doctrine you've been followin'. But steer clear of grody, made-up old wives' tales. Train yourself toward godliness instead. Physical trainin's got some value, sure, but godliness has value across the board, holdin' the promise of life right now, and the life that's still comin'.

web_text: This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance. For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we have set our trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe. Command and teach these things. Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.
slang_text: This sayin's trustworthy, worth acceptin' all the way. That's exactly why we labor and put up with grief, 'cause we've put our trust in the livin' God, who's the Savior of everybody, especially believers. Order and teach this stuff. Don't let nobody dis you for bein' young — just be an example for believers, in what you say, how you live, in love, in spirit, in faith, in purity.

web_text: Until I come, pay attention to reading, to exhortation, and to teaching. Don’t neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the elders. Be diligent in these things. Give yourself wholly to them, that your progress may be revealed to all. Pay attention to yourself, and to your teaching. Continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.
slang_text: Till I get there, stay focused on readin', encouragin', and teachin'. Don't neglect the gift that's in you, the one you got through prophecy, with the elders layin' hands on you. Be diligent about all this. Throw yourself into it completely, so everybody can see you growin'. Watch yourself close, and watch your teachin' too. Keep at it, 'cause doin' this is gonna save both you and everybody who hears you.

web_text:
Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers; the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, in all purity. Honor widows who are widows indeed. But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show piety towards their own family, and to repay their parents, for this is
 acceptable in the sight of God.
slang_text: Don't rip into an older guy — encourage him like he's your own dad; treat younger guys like brothers; older women like moms; younger women like sisters, totally pure about it. Honor widows who are genuinely widows. But if a widow's got kids or grandkids, let 'em learn first to show respect to their own family and pay back their parents, 'cause that's exactly what pleases God.

web_text: Now she who is a widow indeed, and desolate, has her hope set on God, and continues in petitions and prayers night and day. But she who gives herself to pleasure is dead while she lives. Also command these things, that they may be without reproach. But if anyone doesn’t provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.
slang_text: Now a widow who's genuinely alone's got her hope pinned on God, stayin' at it with prayers and requests, night and day. But the one who's chasin' pleasure is basically dead while she's still breathin'. Lay these orders down too, so they stay beyond reproach. But if anybody won't provide for their own family, especially their own household, they've flat-out denied the faith and turned out worse than somebody who never believed at all.

web_text: Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man, being approved by good works, if she has brought up children, if she has been hospitable to strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, and if she has diligently followed every good work. But refuse younger widows, for when they have grown wanton against Christ, they desire to marry; having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.
slang_text: Don't let nobody get put on the widows' list who's under sixty, and she's gotta have been faithful to one husband, proven out through good works — raisin' kids, bein' hospitable to strangers, washin' the saints' feet, helpin' out the hurtin', chasin' after every kinda good work she can find. But turn away the younger widows, 'cause once they start gettin' restless against Christ, they wanna get married again, endin' up condemned for tossin' aside their first pledge.

web_text: Besides, they also learn to be idle, going about from house to house. Not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not. I desire therefore that the younger widows marry, bear children, rule the household, and give no occasion to the adversary for insulting. For already some have turned away after Satan. If any man or woman who believes has widows, let them relieve them, and don’t let the assembly be burdened; that it might relieve those who are widows indeed.
slang_text: On top of that, they get used to bein' lazy, wanderin' house to house. Not just lazy, either — gossips and busybodies, sayin' stuff they got no business sayin'. So I want the younger widows to get married, have kids, run their household, and not give Satan a single openin' to trash-talk us. 'Cause some have already gone chasin' after Satan. If any believin' man or woman has widows in their own family, let them take care of 'em, and don't burden the church with it, so the church can take care of the widows who are genuinely alone.

web_text:
Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching. For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.” And,
“The laborer is worthy of his wages.” Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses. Those who sin, reprove in the sight of all, that the rest also may be in fear.
slang_text: Elders who lead well oughta get counted worthy of double honor, especially the ones bustin' their hump preachin' and teachin'. 'Cause Scripture says, "Don't muzzle the ox while it's grindin' out the grain." And, "The worker deserves his wages." Don't take an accusation against an elder unless there's two or three witnesses backin' it up. Whoever's sinnin', call 'em out in front of everybody, so the rest of the crew stays on their toes.

web_text: I command you in the sight of God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the chosen angels, that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing by partiality. Lay hands hastily on no one, neither be a participant in other men’s sins. Keep yourself pure. Be no longer a drinker of water only, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities. Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.
slang_text: I'm orderin' you, right in front of God and the Lord Jesus Christ and the chosen angels, to keep to these rules without playin' favorites, not showin' bias to nobody. Don't rush into layin' hands on anybody, and don't get tangled up in other folks' sins. Keep yourself pure. Don't stick to just drinkin' water — use a little wine for your stomach's sake, since you're sick so often. Some folks' sins are plain to see, showin' up before judgment even hits, and some don't show up till later.

web_text: In the same way also there are good works that are obvious, and those that are otherwise can’t be hidden.
slang_text: Same deal with good works — some are plain obvious, and even the ones that aren't can't stay hidden forever.

web_text: Let as many as are bondservants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the doctrine not be blasphemed. Those who have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brothers, but rather let them serve them, because those who partake of the benefit are believing and beloved. Teach and exhort these things. If anyone teaches a different doctrine, and doesn’t consent to sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness, he is conceited, knowing nothing, but obsessed with arguments, disputes, and word battles, from which come envy, strife, insulting, evil suspicions,
slang_text: Let every servant under the yoke count their own masters worthy of full honor, so God's name and the doctrine don't get blasphemed. Servants with believin' masters shouldn't disrespect 'em just 'cause they're brothers — nah, serve 'em even harder, 'cause the ones benefitin' from that service are believers, beloved even. Teach and push all this. If anybody teaches some different doctrine and won't agree with sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and the doctrine that matches up with godliness, he's just stuck-up, clueless, obsessed with arguments and disputes and word battles, which just breeds envy, fightin', insults, evil suspicions,

web_text: constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such. But godliness with contentment is great gain. For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out. But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.
slang_text: and nonstop friction among corrupt, truth-starved folks who figure godliness is just a hustle for cash. Steer clear of guys like that. But godliness with contentment is a huge score. 'Cause we brought nothin' into this world, and we sure ain't takin' nothin' out. So as long as we got food and clothes, let's be content with that.

web_text: But those who are determined to be rich fall into a temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful lusts, such as drown men in ruin and destruction. For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows. But you, man of God, flee these things, and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness. Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.
slang_text: But guys who are dead-set on gettin' rich fall right into temptation, a trap, a bunch of dumb and harmful cravings that drown people in ruin and destruction. 'Cause lovin' money's the root of all kinds of evil. Some folks got led astray from the faith chasin' greed, and stabbed themselves through with a ton of grief. But you, man of God, run from all that, and chase after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, gentleness. Fight the good fight of faith. Grab onto the eternal life you got called to, the one you already confessed loud and clear in front of a ton of witnesses.

web_text: I command you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate testified the good confession, that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ; which in its own times he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and Lord of lords; who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light; whom no man has seen, nor can see: to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.
slang_text: I'm orderin' you, right in front of God, who gives life to everything, and Christ Jesus, who testified the good confession right in front of Pontius Pilate, to keep this commandment spotless, blameless, right up till our Lord Jesus Christ shows back up — which He's gonna reveal right on time, He who's the one and only blessed Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords; the only one with immortality, livin' in light nobody can even get close to; nobody's ever seen Him, nobody can. To Him be honor and eternal power. Amen.

web_text: Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be haughty, nor have their hope set on the uncertainty of riches, but on the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy; that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to share; laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life. Timothy, guard that which is committed to you, turning away from the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge;
slang_text: Order the folks who are rich in this world right now not to get stuck-up, and not to pin their hope on money, which is unreliable anyway, but on the livin' God, who loads us up rich with everything to enjoy. Tell 'em to do good, get rich in good works, be ready to hand stuff out, stay generous — stackin' up a solid foundation for themselves, ready for what's comin', so they can grab onto eternal life. Timothy, guard what's been handed to you, turnin' away from empty chatter and arguments over what's falsely called knowledge;

web_text: which some profess, and thus have wandered from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
slang_text: which some folks claim to have, and by doin' that, wandered clean away from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
